content_markdown: The greenhouse effect can be explained by the fact that the infrared radiation emitted by the surface of Earth

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

options.label: A
options.text: is absorbed by the atmosphere and then re-radiated in all directions.

options.label: B
options.text: raises the temperature of the upper atmosphere.

options.label: C
options.text: is trapped by the upper atmosphere.

options.label: D
options.text: is absorbed by the atmosphere and then all of it is re-radiated back to the surface of Earth.

vibe_explanation: The greenhouse effect occurs because greenhouse gases in the atmosphere absorb long-wavelength infrared radiation emitted from the Earth's surface. These molecules then re-emit that energy in all directions (isotropically), including back towards the surface, which leads to an increase in the average temperature of the Earth. Option D is incorrect because not 'all' of it goes back to the surface; some is radiated out to space.
